targets.sourceTitle: 'ABU will continue to support individuals and groups in advancing AI in learning'

expectedThemes: AI teaching and learning strategy source status

stopConditions: Stop after official ABU AI-specific policy/guidance sources and adjacent official policy context have been searched, fetched, and classified.
Do not crawl or stage unofficial blogs, repository scholarship, vendor pages, media summaries, or ranking pages as claim evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:abu.edu.ng Ahmadu Bello University artificial intelligence generative AI ChatGPT policy academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl official-domain query returned no web results for a public ABU generative-AI or ChatGPT policy.
Firecrawl returned an official ABU AI challenges page plus repository scholarship and external research pages; only official ABU pages were retained.

methodsAttempted.query: site:abu.edu.ng "ChatGPT" OR "Generative AI" OR "Artificial Intelligence" "Ahmadu Bello University"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl returned official ABU AI news and programme pages, but no public student generative-AI rule or AI tools data-handling policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:abu.edu.ng Ahmadu Bello University plagiarism policy academic integrity students assessment misconduct PDF
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl found the official ABU Research Policy PDF with plagiarism, misconduct, research compliance, and data-retention language.
Direct robots.txt fetch showed Disallow: /wp-admin/ and two sitemap entries; all staged sources are public pages or PDFs outside /wp-admin/.

artifacts.summary: Official ABU searches and fetches found AI-related official news pages and an official research policy PDF. The run did not find a public official ABU generative-AI use policy, approved/prohibited AI tools list, or AI-tool data/security policy; claims are therefore scoped to source-status, teaching/learning strategy context, and adjacent research-integrity policy text.

artifacts.claimText: The crawl found official ABU pages discussing AI strategies and teaching/learning context, but did not find a public official ABU generative-AI use policy, approved/prohibited AI tools list, or AI-tool data/security policy.

citation.citationTitle: How to navigate challenges posed by Artificial Intelligence in ABU, by Vice-Chancellor

artifacts.claimText: Official ABU news pages say university leaders discussed updating teaching and learning policy for AI transparency/accountability and later stated that ABU had put strategies in place for use of AI in teaching and learning; the pages do not publish the full strategy or student-use rules.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence key pillar in ABU's strategic goal, says Vice-Chancellor

artifacts.claimText: An official ABU conference news page reports a keynote statement that tools like ChatGPT can complete assignments instantly and challenge assessment and originality, but it does not state an ABU rule permitting or prohibiting student AI-tool use.

citation.citationTitle: 'ABU will continue to support individuals and groups in advancing AI in learning'

artifacts.claimText: ABU's Research Policy is described as university-wide for research practice and defines plagiarism as research ethical misconduct; this is adjacent research-integrity context and not AI-specific guidance.

ABU's Research Policy assigns responsibility for research data collection, management, storage, and retention in research projects; the source does not state AI-tool-specific privacy or security rules.

artifacts.sourceTitle: How to navigate challenges posed by Artificial Intelligence in ABU, by Vice-Chancellor
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Bala stressed that ABU governance, teachers, and other stakeholders must proactively engage in updating the teaching and learning policy to include strategies and guidelines that ensure transparency and accountability in the use of artificial intelligence technologies and tools in the teaching, learning, and research processes.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Article body, ABU Public Affairs Directorate page dated 12 December 2023

artifacts.sourceTitle: Ahmadu Bello University Research Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The ABU Research Policy is a university wide policy and these guidelines should be seen as a framework for sound research practice and for the protection of individual research workers, including both staff and postgraduate research students, from possible misunderstanding.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Ahmadu Bello University Research Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Plagiarism is a form of ethical misconduct in research and consists of the intentional or unintentional "use of the words, ideas, diagrams of publicly available work without appropriately acknowledging the sources of these materials."

artifacts.sourceTitle: Ahmadu Bello University Research Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Data collection, management and storage shall be the responsibility of the PI in case of group research. In case of student research (UG and PG), the responsibility of the data safety and retention is that of the student before graduation.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Research Policy, research data collection/archiving section

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim; no public official ABU generative-AI use policy or AI-tool rule was found in the official-source search.
Approved only as teaching/learning strategy context; evidence does not publish detailed ABU student-use rules.
Approved as context about a reported conference statement; it is not an ABU prohibition or approval rule for ChatGPT.
Approved as adjacent research-integrity context; explicitly not AI-specific.
Approved as adjacent research data-handling context only; no AI-tool privacy/security rule was found.

artifacts.title: Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria AI Policy Source Status
artifacts.summary: Official ABU sources found during this crawl discuss AI in teaching and learning, policy-development strategies, and AI as a strategic priority. The crawl did not find a public official ABU generative-AI use policy, AI-tool approval/prohibition list, or AI-tool-specific privacy/security rule. ABU's official Research Policy provides adjacent university-wide research-integrity and data-handling context, including plagiarism and data storage responsibilities.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The official ABU AI pages found were news/context pages and did not publish detailed binding student-use rules.
Research Policy claims are adjacent context only and should not be presented as AI-specific policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
